purpose: Quantifies cross-asset impact of real rate moves to demonstrate asymmetric positioning risk and the no-good-outcome dynamic for US-centric multi-asset portfolios
components: 50bp real rate rise: Mag-7 -12-15%, S&P 500 -6-8%, IG spreads +10-20bp, DXY +2-3%
50bp real rate decline: equities helped but dollar weakened stressing feedback loop
Effective equity duration: Mag-7 at ~25-30 years at 35-40x forward P/E
No-good-outcome asymmetry: every direction hurts at least one portfolio component
current_assessment: At 2.0-2.2% 10Y TIPS real rates are the cross-asset fulcrum. Rising rates hurt equities directly; falling rates help equities but weaken dollar threatening capex-profit-inflow loop. US-centric portfolios face no safe direction under current conditions.
limitations: Sensitivity estimates subject to plus/minus 30% estimation error
Linear sensitivity may break down for moves greater than 100bp
Correlation regime could shift during large rate moves
Does not account for Fed policy response to rate moves
